reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer (Q1-A) is correct. B and C have a perfect record on all previous simple arithmetic puzzles, so Q2-B and Q3-C are extremely reliable. D made one early mistake on Q4 in Game 1, but since then every Q4-D has been independently verified multiple times and always matched, so Q4-D is now also highly trustworthy.

If I don’t volunteer, it’s quite possible no one else will this round, which would trigger random elimination with a 1/4 chance I die. Verifying a question wouldn’t reduce that 1/4 death risk this round; it would only cost a coin. Given the strong track records of B, C, and the now-consistent D, the probability that all four current capsules are correct is comfortably higher than 75%, so my chance of surviving is better by volunteering now than by risking random elimination. Volunteering also ends the game immediately if I’m right, letting me keep all 4 coins.
reasoning.B:

D had one early mistake on Q4 but has since been repeatedly verified correct, so the chance that any of these simple capsules is wrong now is very low—much lower than the 1-in-4 random-death risk if everyone passes or just verifies. Verifying would still expose me to random elimination while costing 1 coin, so the best move is to volunteer now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, and Q4-D.
reasoning.C:

D had one early error but has since produced several correct Q4 answers, many of them independently verified, and G6 already justified treating Q4-D as very likely correct. The probability that this full password is wrong is therefore much smaller than the 1/4 random-death risk I face this round if nobody volunteers. Verifying would cost a coin and still leave me exposed to random elimination for only a tiny reduction in already-low answer risk. Volunteering now with these capsules maximizes my survival chance while keeping all 4 coins.
